connection: Add new strategies without modifying the Context. This is the CORE reason Strategy exists.
example: Adding CryptoPayment required ZERO changes to PaymentProcessor. We only ADDED a new class.

connection: Context depends on the Strategy interface (abstraction), not concrete classes.
Any concrete strategy is substitutable in the Context. Swap CreditCard for PayPal and Context works identically.
example: processor.setStrategy(new CreditCardPayment(...)) and processor.setStrategy(new PayPalPayment(...)) -- Context doesn't care which.

connection: Each strategy class has exactly one job -- its own algorithm. CreditCardPayment doesn't know about PayPal.
example: Validation logic for credit cards lives ONLY in CreditCardPayment, not spread across the Context.
